chunk:
CHUNK:017/021
And then, when Sean and me were gone from one day to the next, they had a few funny stories about that man who printed his name in all the guestbooks and left them with the most amazing jewellery that just had to be fake, because no sane man would ever leave real diamonds in the hands of strangers.\u201d \u201cOr pockets,\u201d I whispered as Rachael turned her back on me and ripped one page after another out of the guestbook. She stuffed the pages into the inside pocket of her jacket and then picked up the MP5. \u201cI wasn\u2019t so sure about Sean being sane and I told him so when we jumped ship and flew to Copenhagen. He told me we\u2019d catch up with our new friends once they got back to the States. And then, since they had been so kind to print their names and addresses in the guestbook, it would be easy enough to find them, retrieve the diamonds they had smuggled into America, and cash in.\u201d Rachael sighed and said, \u201cOnly it didn\u2019t work out that way.\u201d \u201cBecause Sean didn\u2019t have the guestbook, did he?\u201d \u201cNo,\u201d Rachael said. \u201cThat was my job. I was supposed to be last to sign the book. And then I was supposed to rip the page out when no one was looking. But I was young \u2013 too young to be discreet or even confident about that kind of thing, and, well\u2026\u201d Rachael shrugged. \u201cSo, you came back.\u201d \u201cYeah,\u201d she said. \u201cJust a couple of years later.\u201d Rachael looked up at the sound of a soft thumping noise that filtered through the turf roof of the longhouse. \u201cBetter late than never, I suppose,\u201d she said. \u201cYes,\u201d I said, although as the thumping noise grew louder, I wondered if time had run out for Rachael Silk and Sean Arnet, America\u2019s Most Wanted. \n 13 I wanted to ask if Sean was doing the same thing again, only this time he didn\u2019t bother to make friends, just dropped the jewellery into the passengers\u2019 pockets, but the thumping sound was louder and even through the turf roof, the sound of a helicopter was unmistakable. \u201cWe should go,\u201d I said, thinking that Rachael had said errands, plural, and that Gaba might have let her take me, so that I might see it through to the end. \u201cWhy?\u201d \u201cYou can\u2019t hear it?\u201d I pointed at the roof. \u201cThat\u2019s a helicopter. The Americans are coming, and you\u2019re running out of time.\u201d \u201cThat\u2019s sweet,\u201d Rachael said. \u201cBut you\u2019re wrong, Petra. I have plenty of time. And if I need more, they\u2019ll give it to me.\u201d She shifted her grip on the MP5 and then nodded at the door, leaving me to guess whom she might be working for, or with whom she had struck a deal. Not Gaba, I thought. Although he had had plenty of time while I was on land chasing down Paniula\u2019s diary. That thought stopped me cold, and I halted halfway down the length of the longhouse, oblivious to Rachael\u2019s prodding and prompting, as I wondered if it was all a ruse, that Uiluiit had lied, or, worse, that Gaba had somehow set me up, knowing that I might be finished too quickly with a jag of Greenlandic culture, and return to the ship before he had finished making his deal with Rachael. \u201cThen it\u2019s a fake,\u201d I whispered. \u201cSomething Uiluiit planted in the cabin and scribbled my name inside to make me think it really was the ramblings of a disgruntled and convicted police officer.\u201d I pulled the diary out of my pocket and opened it to the first page. I ignored the thump of the helicopter and Rachael\u2019s chiding as I found my name on the first page, holding my breath as I read the words around it, heart pounding as I found the names Paniula preferred to call me by, together with the name Venus Manumina, and that I was his Venus. Like father, like son, like\u2026 \u201cI\u2019ll take that,\u201d Rachael said as she plucked the diary from my hands. I started to protest, but she simply raised the submachine gun and pointed the barrel at my chest. \u201cI don\u2019t know what you\u2019ve got here, honey, but it\u2019ll keep these pages flat,\u201d she said, setting the diary down on the floor and crouching beside it as she pulled the pages of the guestbook out of her pocket and tucked them inside the diary. \u201cThere,\u201d she said. Rachael slipped Paniula\u2019s diary into her jacket pocket and stood up. \u201cGive it back,\u201d I said. I held out my hand. \u201cGive it back or you can forget about me driving you anywhere else in the boat.\u201d \u201cPetra,\u201d Rachael said. \u201cYou were doing so well, and now you\u2019re getting razzled again.\u201d She moved to one side to step around me, but I blocked her. \u201cGive it back,\u201d I said. \u201cHoney?\u201d Rachael shook her head. \u201cThis isn\u2019t you. So emotional.\u201d She took a step back, clacking her nails against the MP5 as she studied me. \u201cWhat is it? Why is this book so important to you?\u201d I couldn\u2019t explain it if I tried. I had yet to read more than a few lines. But if the diary was genuine, and I thought it was too elaborate not to be, then it was a piece of my history, connected to a difficult episode in my past. Sure, we were standing in a reconstructed Viking longhouse, something from Greenland\u2019s past. But it was so old, I could never really relate to it. The diary in Rachael\u2019s pocket, however, was new. It was recent. And it was about me. \u201cPetra,\u201d Rachael said. She licked her lips as I took a step towards her. \u201cYou need to think about this, honey. You need to think real hard, now. Because\u2026\u201d She took a step back. \u201cBecause when I told you I never meant to hurt you, I meant it.\u201d I took another step forward, and Rachael moved another step back.
compressed_context:
CTX: LONGHOUSE-INTERIOR

ACTION: RACHAEL TURNS AWAY; RIPS GUESTBOOK-PAGES; STOWS IN JACKET-POCKET
ACTION: HOLDS MP5

PETRA: "FUNNY-STORIES ABOUT A MAN PRINTING HIS NAME IN GUESTBOOK; LEAVING AMAZING JEWELLERY→FAKE"
PETRA: "NO SANE MAN LEAVES REAL DIAMONDS WITH STRANGERS"
PETRA: [WHISPER] "OR POCKETS"

RACHAEL: "I WASN'T SURE SEAN WAS SANE; TOLD HIM SO WHEN WE FLED TO COPENHAGEN"
RACHAEL: "HE SAID WE'D CATCH UP WITH NEW-FRIENDS BACK IN STATES"
RACHAEL: "GUESTBOOK NAMES+ADDRESSES→EASY DIAMOND-RETRIEVAL+CASH-IN"
RACHAEL: "ONLY IT DIDN'T WORK OUT THAT WAY."

PETRA: "BECAUSE SEAN DIDN'T HAVE THE GUESTBOOK, DID HE?"
RACHAEL: "NO. THAT WAS MY JOB."
RACHAEL: "I WAS LAST TO SIGN; THEN RIP PAGE WHEN NO-ONE LOOKED"
RACHAEL: "BUT I WAS TOO YOUNG→NEITHER DISCREET NOR CONFIDENT"

PETRA: "SO YOU CAME BACK?"
RACHAEL: "YEARS LATER."

CTX: HELICOPTER-SOUND↑ THROUGH TURF-ROOF
PETRA: "WE SHOULD GO; CAN'T YOU HEAR IT?"
PETRA: "THAT'S A HELICOPTER. AMERICANS COMING; YOU'RE RUNNING OUT OF TIME"

RACHAEL: "THAT'S SWEET; BUT YOU'RE WRONG, PETRA"
RACHAEL: "I HAVE PLENTY OF TIME; THEY'LL GIVE MORE IF NEEDED" [NODS AT DOOR]

CTX: PETRA HALTS MID-LONGHOUSE AISLE; WONDERS IF GABA SET HER UP ∨ UILUIIT LIED
PETRA: [WHISPER] "THEN IT'S FAKE; UILUIIT PLANTED IT IN CABIN"
PETRA: [WHISPER] "SCRIBBLED MY NAME TO FAKE RAMBLINGS OF CONVICTED POLICE-OFFICER"

ACTION: PETRA PULLS DIARY FROM POCKET; OPENS TO FIRST-PAGE
ACTION: PETRA FINDS HER NAME ∧ VENUS-MANUMINA; HEART POUNDING

RACHAEL: "I'LL TAKE THAT"; PLUCKS DIARY FROM PETRA

ACTION: RACHAEL RAISES MP5→PETRA; SETS DIARY DOWN; CROUCHES BESIDE IT
ACTION: PULLS GUESTBOOK-PAGES FROM POCKET; TUCKS THEM INSIDE DIARY
ACTION: SLIPS DIARY IN JACKET-POCKET; STANDS

PETRA: "GIVE IT BACK OR FORGET ABOUT ME DRIVING YOU ANYWHERE ELSE IN BOAT"

RACHAEL: "PETRA, YOU WERE DOING SO WELL; NOW YOU'RE GETTING RAZZLED"
ACTION: RACHAEL MOVES TO STEP AROUND PETRA; PETRA BLOCKS

PETRA: "GIVE IT BACK"
PETRA: "HONEY?"

RACHAEL: "THIS ISN'T YOU. SO EMOTIONAL."
ACTION: RACHAEL CLICKS NAILS ON MP5; STUDIES PETRA

RACHAEL: "WHAT IS IT? WHY IS THIS BOOK SO IMPORTANT TO YOU?"

CTX: PETRA CAN'T EXPLAIN; DIARY IS PIECE OF HER HISTORY CONNECTED TO PAST EPISODE

RACHAEL: "YOU NEED TO THINK REAL-HARD, HONEY; WHEN I SAID I NEVER MEANT TO HURT YOU, I MEANT IT."
ACTION: PETRA STEPS FORWARD ∧ RACHAEL STEPS BACK
